title: Turbiditic trench deposits at the South-Chilean active margin: A Pleistocene–Holocene record of climate and tectonics
abstract: Sedimentary records from ODP Site 1232 and SONNE core 50SL in the Peru-Chile trench at ~40°S and ~38°S document frequent turbidites interpreted as seismically triggered paleoseismic indicators. During sea level highstands in the Holocene and MIS 5, recurrence times of turbidite layers are substantially higher, reflecting climate-induced reduction of sediment availability. Glacial turbidite recurrence times are within the same order of magnitude as earthquake recurrence times from historical records.

location.description: Chile Trench at ~38°–40°S; ODP Site 1232 and R/V SONNE core sites; turbidite paleoseismicity record

seismic_lines.name: SONNE sub-bottom profiler and seismic reflection profiles — south Chile trench ~38°–40°S

data.name: SONNE MCS/Parasound Profiles — South Chile Trench ~38°–40°S
data.description: Seismic reflection and subbottom profiler data from R/V SONNE cruises in the Chile Trench (~38°–40°S) used as stratigraphic framework for core site selection and turbidite interpretation

data.name: Parasound Sub-Bottom Profiles — South Chile Trench
data.description: Parasound sub-bottom profiler data from R/V SONNE used to image shallow stratigraphy and select core sites in the Chile Trench

data.name: Swath Bathymetry — South Chile Trench ~38°–40°S
data.description: Multibeam bathymetry from SONNE cruises in the south Chile Trench region

_data_note: Primarily a sedimentological paper; seismic reflection and Parasound profiles used for site selection framework. ODP Site 1232 data available via ODP TAMU.

processing.summary: Sub-bottom profiler (Parasound) and multichannel seismic reflection profiles from R/V SONNE were used to define the stratigraphic framework and select core sites in the Chile Trench at ~38°–40°S. The primary analysis focuses on sediment cores (ODP Site 1232 and piston core 50SL) for turbidite paleoseismicity records. Seismic stratigraphy provides context for core-to-core correlation.
workflow: 1. Parasound sub-bottom profiling for shallow imaging
2. MCS profiling for deeper trench fill geometry
processing.notes: Primarily a sedimentological study; seismic data serves as auxiliary framework

analysis_notes: EPSL paper; note the authors list in survey_results.json uses different names (Blumberg et al.) than the title attribution. Behind paywall. Seismic role is secondary to core analysis.
